spec_match.rationale: Implements the cross-iframe propagation the spec was designed for: parent calls `setPreferredSinkId`, child iframe's `<audio>.sinkId` updates without a postMessage handshake.

interactive.rationale: Live select in the parent + live readout of `audio.sinkId` in a same-origin iframe; pressing play in either pane streams the tone to whichever device the parent chose.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `parent frame's preferred-sink choice flows into child iframes' audio elements without postMessage. Pick a sink in the parent — the iframe's audio.sinkId updates live`. Page is exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Same-origin iframe is covered, but the chromestatus summary explicitly highlights `cross-origin child frames` — the page does not demonstrate that case (which would require a second origin in the server, or a sandboxed iframe). Also no permissions-policy `speaker-selection` consideration.

references.rationale: `see also` cites the chromestatus link and the mediacapture-output spec.

design_system.rationale: 52 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Add a cross-origin iframe demo
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary's headline phrase is `including cross-origin child frames`. Currently only the same-origin iframe is demonstrated. Add a second iframe served from a different origin (server route needed) so the cross-origin propagation case is real, not implied.

openQuestions.title: Cover the permissions-policy `speaker-selection` case
openQuestions.detail: An iframe with `allow="speaker-selection 'none'"` should NOT inherit the parent's preferred sink. Add a sandboxed/disallowed iframe alongside the inheriting one so the policy gate is visible.

openQuestions.title: Show what happens when the iframe owns a sinkId already
openQuestions.detail: If the iframe has already called `audio.setSinkId(...)` for one of its elements, does the parent's `setPreferredSinkId` override it or not? Add a `iframe overrides` toggle to surface the precedence rule.

summary: Covers the spec's flagship `parent-frame routes audio for itself plus children without IPC` story with a real working iframe, but only same-origin — the chromestatus summary's explicit cross-origin claim is unverified by the demo. Adding a cross-origin iframe + a permissions-policy-blocked iframe would make this the canonical reference page.
